stars.description: JAX in JavaScript – ML library for the web, running on WebGPU & Wasm
stars.highlights: JAX in JavaScript brings JAX's ML capabilities to web environments, enabling GPU-accelerated machine learning directly in browsers via WebGPU and WebAssembly. It provides familiar JAX APIs for neural networks and numerical computing, making ML accessible without server-side dependencies.
stars.worthReading: This project demonstrates innovative web-native ML infrastructure by bridging JAX's powerful abstractions with modern browser capabilities, offering a glimpse into the future of client-side AI applications.
stars.scoreReason: Freshness: Young repo with rising traction (784 stars) and modern WebGPU/Wasm approach. Relevance: High - brings JAX/ML to web frontier. Concreteness: Concrete implementation of JAX in JavaScript with specific technical details.

xPosts.content: Attention and RNNs are distinct, but Linear Attention is a Linear RNN, but not vice versa.
xPosts.highlights: Clarifies the relationship between attention mechanisms and RNNs, noting that linear attention can be viewed as a linear RNN, but the reverse is not true.
xPosts.worthReading: Provides a technical insight into the mathematical connections between different neural network architectures.

xPosts.content: #acl2020nlp Lot of threads online about likes and dislikes for the conference. Twitter is fleeting, github is forever. Send issues or PRs: https://github.com/Mini-Conf/Mini-Conf/issues… It's early days, we're making up virtual conferences as we go along.
xPosts.highlights: Sasha Rush advocates for using GitHub over Twitter for lasting conference feedback, and acknowledges the experimental nature of virtual conferences.
xPosts.worthReading: Highlights the shift to virtual conferences and the value of persistent open-source contributions.

xPosts.content: (My last chance to tweet about Yoon Kim as he leaves the lab 😢. Part of an amazing group of students.) Congrats to Yoon on winning this year's HarvardCS thesis award! And since its public, Yoon is heading next to MIT. Highly recommend sending an app🍎 https://seas.
xPosts.highlights: Sasha Rush congratulates student Yoon Kim on winning a thesis award and announces his move to MIT.
xPosts.worthReading: Shows Rush's mentorship and pride in his students' achievements.

xPosts.content: Congrats to Dr. Yoon Kim 🍾 who zoom defended his dissertation "Deep Latent Variable Model of Natural Language". Yoon's research is wonderful, he's also such a thoughtful teacher and dedicated collaborator. Very curious what he decides to do next
xPosts.highlights: Sasha Rush celebrates Yoon Kim's PhD defense and praises his research and character.
xPosts.worthReading: Demonstrates Rush's support for students and recognition of their contributions.

xPosts.content: Composer is a new model we built at Cursor. We used RL to train a big MoE model to be really good at real-world coding, and also very fast. https://cursor.com/blog/composer Excited for the potential of building specialized models to help in critical domains.
xPosts.highlights: Sasha Rush announces Composer, a new RL-trained MoE coding model from Cursor, emphasizing speed and real-world coding performance.
xPosts.worthReading: Showcases a practical application of RL and MoE for specialized coding assistance.

interestSummary: Sasha Rush has recently joined the Cursor team and is focused on AI-powered code development tools, while also engaging in debates about Transformer architectures and showing interest in running machine learning models in the browser via WebGPU.
